Settings

Tune Arca’s timer, AI, and core app behavior without touching tasks or goals.

Work & break lengths

These settings shape the default pomodoro cycle used in Execute and Focus mode. The 4-block session loop stays fixed for now.

Focus & alerts

The fullscreen focus overlay remains available with your existing keyboard flow.
Desktop alerts follow the current browser/app permission.

Sound

Use Test Sound to audition the current tone at the current profile.
Current sound behavior applies everywhere the timer ends.

AI

Disabled
Local AI Setup

Advanced
AI is off. Enable above.
Use Arca AI in the sidebar for normal interaction. This section is for provider setup, connection testing, and reviewing structured changes.

Proposed Changes

Core modules

Keep the daily shell simple by leaving experimental modules off until you want to test them.

Health (alpha)
Hidden by default until enabled.
Wealth (alpha)
Hidden by default until enabled.

Setup & appearance

Replay the setup flow safely, and keep future structure/settings work in one place.

Dark mode and deeper structure settings belong here next, but are not live in this first pass.

Keyboard shortcuts

Open settingsJump to Settings > AI from anywhere in the app.
⌘,
Focus AI inputOpen the most relevant AI composer for the current surface.
⌘K
Start or resume focusStarts the timer if idle, or resumes and enters Focus mode.
⌘↩
Pause or resume timerToggles the active timer without leaving the keyboard flow.
Space
Expand windowToggles the same native expand action as the green window button.
F

Storage status

Native store Arca is using its current desktop data source.
Desktop
Backup, restore, and full storage diagnostics still live in the existing App & Data panel for now.

Open existing tools

This first pass keeps the mature backup and sync flows intact, and exposes them from Settings instead of making you hunt for them.

What comes next

Later passes should move backup, restore, storage mode, and cloud credentials directly into this section and retire the old modal.

Local AI Setup

Detect Ollama, download a local model, and configure Arca to keep requests on this machine.

Welcome to Arca

Set up in under 5 minutes
1. Welcome 2. AI assist (optional) 3. Primary goal 4. Life practices 5. First tasks 6. Start
Welcome

Set up the same operating system you will use every day.

Arca keeps one primary goal visible, protects time for high-value work, and gives you structured health, wealth, and review surfaces without turning the app into a cockpit on day one.

Execute Capture tasks, run focus blocks, and keep Today grounded in what actually matters.
Plan Keep goals, commitments, review signals, and coach support in one planning surface.
Health Track exercise, recovery, nutrition, and body signals only when they become relevant.
Wealth Bring cash flow, portfolio, and property data into one place with AI-ready context.
Choose your path

Start with the full board, or just test AI.

Full setup walks through your goal, practices, and first tasks. AI-only mode is the safe path if you want to test providers or set up Ollama without touching the rest of your board.

Full setup creates the first usable version of your board: goal, practices, and tasks. Or skip and explore freely — you can run setup any time from Settings.

Framework: one primary goal, controllable inputs, a visible scoreboard, and review loops that keep drift obvious before it becomes a lost month.

AI assist

Choose whether Arca should use AI coaching.

AI can help prioritise tasks, coach life practices, and generate a starter setup from a short brain-dump. When active, it sees your goals, board columns (including Done Today), practices, and commitments. It does not see passwords, financial accounts, or data from other apps.

AI provider

Choose cloud or local AI.

Cloud AI is fastest to start. Ollama keeps prompts on your machine. You can change providers later in settings.

Cloud AI

Use a major foundation model via API. Fast and capable. Your prompts are sent to the provider you choose. Review that provider's current privacy and retention terms before use.

DeepSeek's API sends data to servers in China where national security laws can compel data handover. For privacy, consider running DeepSeek locally via Ollama instead.

Local AI (Ollama) — 100% private

Run an AI model directly on your machine. Nothing leaves your computer. Start with Llama, then optionally add Qwen, Mistral, or DeepSeek locally via Ollama.

The setup wizard can detect Ollama, download a starter model, and configure Arca to use it locally.
Starter model: Llama 3.2. Optional local upgrades: Qwen 7B, Mistral Small 3.1, DeepSeek R1.
Starter setup

Describe what matters right now.

Set up later? No problem. Press Next and continue manual onboarding.

How Arca works: focus on one primary goal, track controllable inputs, keep a visible scoreboard, and review weekly so priorities stay honest.

Primary goal

If you could only achieve one thing in the next 30–90 days, what would it be?

Choose something that would make everything else easier or more meaningful. A good goal is specific, measurable, and time-bound — think "From X to Y by when."

Strong goal framing
  • Outcome first: define the specific result you want, not the vague area of life.
  • Time-bounded: give it a date that creates pressure without fantasy.
  • Observable: your measure should make it obvious whether you actually got there.

I'll set my primary goal later

Life practices

The daily habits that keep you grounded.

Not everything important is a work goal. These are recurring personal practices — meditation, exercise, reading, music. Frequency matters more than ambition. Set a minimum you can actually hit every day.

Keep this realistic
  • Minimums beat ideals: pick a floor you can maintain even on compressed days.
  • Daily is strongest: recurring practices work best when they are easy to score.
  • Use notes sparingly: only add context that will matter in weekly review.
First tasks

Break your goal into concrete tasks for this week.

Strategic tasks directly advance your primary goal — the work that predicts whether you'll hit the target. Maintenance is everything else: emails, admin, meetings. It needs doing, but shouldn't crowd out strategic work.

Good first-week mix
  • Start with 3 strategic tasks: enough to move the goal without overpromising the week.
  • Use Today deliberately: only pin work that genuinely deserves attention immediately.
  • Deep vs shallow matters: the app becomes more honest when you classify task depth well.
Preview

Review your setup before launching.

Goal
Practices
Tasks
Reset warning: this will replace tasks, sessions, notes, daily logs, weekly reviews, and streak history with a fresh board.
A JSON backup is exported automatically before reset. Restore via Import JSON File.
What happens next
  • Board mode: the app creates your first goal, practices, and tasks immediately.
  • AI-only mode: you finish with provider setup and keep the rest of your existing board untouched.
  • Reset mode: a backup is created before replacement so you can recover if needed.
Inspired by the Eisenhower matrix, deep work principles, and goal-focused execution methods.  Further reading →
